findings.title: Perfect-matching count package for monochromatic quantum graphs
findings.target_need: reusable perfect-matching counting lemmas for files built around weighted perfect-matching sums
findings.what_they_gain: A compact follow-up to `Paper/MonochromaticQuantumGraph.lean` that adds exact odd/even obstruction lemmas and reusable perfect-matching counts, while keeping the full proof in Automath and linking it back with `formal_proof using lean4`.
findings.statement: For a finite-to-one map `p : E -> B`, `no_free_involution_on_odd` rules out any fiberwise fixed-point-free involution as soon as one fiber has odd cardinality. When every fiber has size `2 r_b`, the Automath counting theorem `freeInvolutionCount_formula` gives the single-fiber factor `(2 r_b)! / (2^r_b * r_b!)`, i.e. the classical number of perfect matchings on a `2 r_b`-point set; the paper-level theorem promotes this to the product formula over all fibers.
findings.proof_sketch: Identify a fixed-point-free involution on a fiber with a perfect matching on that fiber. Odd fibers cannot admit such a matching. For `2r` points, order the points and quotient by `r!` pair permutations and `2^r` swaps inside pairs. Fiber independence yields the total product count.
findings.novelty_risk: Low mathematical novelty; the gift is direct reuse inside an existing perfect-matching formalization rather than a new theorem.

findings.title: Finite-state lift bound for Zeckendorf graph benchmarks
findings.target_need: finite-state automata or subword-complexity benchmarks with explicit graph certificates
findings.what_they_gain: A paper-backed AMS 68 benchmark family that fits the repo's external-proof workflow, analogous in spirit to `OEIS/6697.lean` but with explicit state-graph and ambiguity-control lemmas.
findings.statement: For the labeled graph `G_m` attached to finite-window Zeckendorf normalization, `phiState_card` gives `|V_m| = 2^(m-1)`, `phiGraph_outdegree_two` gives outdegree `2` at every state, and `labeledPath_card_le_state_card` shows that the number of deterministic lifts of any observed word is at most `|V_m|`.
findings.proof_sketch: The states are `(m-1)`-bit words. Appending `0` or `1` produces the two outgoing edges. A labeled path is determined by its initial state, so the lift set injects into the state set. The bound is therefore immediate once the graph presentation is fixed.
findings.novelty_risk: Moderate; the finite-state model is source-backed and niche enough to be useful, but the target repo may prefer a smaller declarative slice than the full Automath graph package.

findings.title: Fibonacci-cube exact slice and face laws
findings.target_need: hypercube-derived graph families with exact closed formulas, not just isolated small examples
findings.what_they_gain: A ready graph-combinatorics benchmark family that complements the repo's `Q3` and sensitivity files with exact closed formulas, while keeping long proofs outside the target repo via `formal_proof using lean4`.
findings.statement: For the Fibonacci cube on binary words of length `n` with no adjacent `1`s, `coordOneCount_eq_fib_prod` gives the exact size of the `i`-th coordinate-one slice as `Fib(i+1) * Fib(n-i)`. The low-dimensional face numbers satisfy `fibcubeFVector_two_strict_mono` and `fibcubeFVector_three_recurrence`, while the edge counts satisfy the two-sided estimates `fibcubeEdgeCount_ge_fib` and `fibcubeEdgeCount_double_bound`.
findings.proof_sketch: Decompose admissible words by local suffix patterns and use the Fibonacci recursion for no-adjacent-1 languages. Coordinate-one slices split into independent left and right admissible subwords, yielding the exact product formula. Face and edge formulas then follow from the same recurrence plus symmetry.
findings.novelty_risk: Low-to-medium; Fibonacci cubes are classical, but the target repo currently lacks this kind of exact hypercube-derived benchmark family.
